key_insight: The paper builds certified over-approximations of reachable sets for nonlinear reaction--diffusion PDEs by combining FEM discretization, POD reduction, and reachability analysis with explicit error bounds at each step.
engineering_relevance: This gives engineers a practical, parameter-uniform way to verify safety and uncertainty envelopes for PDE-based models without solving the full infinite-dimensional system.
method: Discretize the PDE with finite elements to obtain a finite-dimensional model.
Apply POD to construct a reduced-order model with bounded approximation error.
Run set-based reachability on the ROM and propagate error bounds to certify the original PDE reachable set.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports explicit upper bounds for FEM and ROM approximation errors, and demonstrates the framework on the Allen--Cahn equation and a logistic growth PDE.
watch_item: The certification depends on the quality of the FEM and POD approximations, so coarse meshes or too aggressive reduction may weaken the reachable-set bounds.
paper_title: Certified Reachable Sets for Nonlinear Reaction--Diffusion Systems
